description: Baseline parity run failed because rubocop exits non-zero when offenses exist; command did not tolerate expected lint exit status.

asi.hypothesis: Measure offense-count parity vs rubocop and warm runtime in one command.
asi.rollback_reason: Experiment command treated expected lint exit code 1 as failure.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun with rubocop/nitrocop exit codes normalized (append `|| true`) and parse JSON output files.

description: Baseline measurement after crash-recovery: nitrocop count 3230 vs rubocop 3153 (delta 77), warm runtime 26.3ms (<100ms) but parity not met.

asi.hypothesis: Establish parity and warm runtime on target repo with preview enabled.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric offense_count_delta is worse than target (77 > 0).
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply syntax-diagnostic dedup fix and add regression tests, then rerun parity measurement.

description: Applied syntax recovery filtering + tests, but parity command still showed delta 77 due stale result-cache reuse; warm runtime stayed 25ms.

asi.hypothesis: Filtering secondary Prism syntax diagnostics should reduce over-count to match RuboCop.
asi.rollback_reason: Measured primary metric unchanged (77) because cached diagnostics masked code change.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun parity check with --no-cache for correctness metric, then separately measure warm cached runtime.
asi.learned: Nitrocop result cache can preserve prior offense totals across binary changes for same files.

description: Implemented syntax-diagnostic recovery filtering (drop secondary '..., ignoring it' at same location when primary exists), added two linter regression tests, and restored rubocop parity: 3153 vs 3153 with warm runtime 25.59ms.

asi.hypothesis: Prism recovery emits secondary syntax diagnostics that RuboCop does not count; suppressing those should recover offense-count parity without runtime regression.

name: nitrocop warm runtime under parity constraint on wt-gph-rspec-rip-out

description: Baseline for warm runtime with parity guard: offense count delta remained 0 and warm cached run measured 26.23ms.

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline warm runtime while enforcing rubocop count parity.

description: Reduced default rayon cap from 8 to 4 threads to lower warm overhead, but warm runtime regressed from 26.23ms to 30.44ms (parity stayed 0).

asi.hypothesis: Lower worker count may reduce scheduling overhead for cache-hit workloads.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened (30.44ms > 26.23ms baseline).
asi.next_action_hint: Try dynamic cap based on file count/cache-hit ratio instead of fixed lower cap.

description: Tried rayon cap=6; parity remained 0 but warm runtime was 26.57ms, slightly worse than 26.23ms baseline.

asi.hypothesis: A moderate cap (6) might balance overhead and parallelism better than 8.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve (26.57ms > 26.23ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep cap=8 and focus on cache-key invalidation/runtime-path optimizations instead.

description: Exploratory thread-count sweep (via RAYON_NUM_THREADS env) found best warm sample at 8 threads (25.51ms) with parity delta 0, but this matched current default behavior and did not represent a code change.

asi.hypothesis: Check whether alternative rayon worker counts can improve warm runtime while preserving parity.
asi.rollback_reason: No implementation change was made; observed improvement is likely sampling noise and not a durable optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Validate noise floor with repeated default measurements before accepting runtime gains.

description: Repeated default warm measurement with parity guard (delta 0) averaged 27.39ms across 8 samples, indicating prior 25.51ms observation was noise.

asi.hypothesis: Re-run default warm timing to validate whether prior apparent improvement is real.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed vs baseline and contradicted prior run, confirming high measurement noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Use median-of-many samples and avoid tiny deltas (<~1ms) for keep decisions.

description: Measured warm runtime noise and stderr skip-summary impact without code changes: baseline avg 25.33ms vs 25.68ms with --quiet-skips; parity remained 0.

asi.hypothesis: If skip-summary stderr emission is a measurable overhead, suppressing it for machine output might improve warm runtime.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; observed delta is tiny and likely noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Pursue real code-path optimizations (cache key/versioning or execution-path pruning), not CLI-flag variance.

description: Added cache session invalidation salt from current binary signature (path+size+mtime) so result-cache entries are isolated across rebuilds; parity stayed exact (delta 0) and warm runtime measured 25.94ms.

asi.hypothesis: Stale result-cache entries across local rebuilds can mask correctness changes; including binary signature in session hash should preserve parity checks and may also improve warm consistency.

description: Confirmation rerun after cache-signature change measured 26.30ms (delta 0), indicating the prior 25.94ms result is within noise and not a reliable warm-runtime improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Re-running the same setup will confirm whether 25.94ms is a stable improvement.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not hold up on confirmation (26.30ms ~ baseline/noise).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the correctness-focused cache-signature commit for stale-cache safety, but seek larger runtime wins before additional keeps.

description: Optimized session-hash construction by avoiding clone+sort when include/exclude vectors are already sorted, but warm runtime regressed to 26.45ms (parity delta stayed 0).

asi.hypothesis: Reducing allocations in compute_session_hash should improve startup/warm runtime.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus baseline (26.45ms > 26.23ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert this micro-optimization and focus on larger hot-path costs outside session-hash setup.

description: Profiling run showed full stat-hit cache behavior (6101 stat hits, 0 misses, debug wall 15ms) but end-to-end warm runtime averaged 26.59ms, no improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Debug phase breakdown may reveal a larger runtime bottleneck to target next.
asi.rollback_reason: No runtime improvement in this profiling-only run.
asi.next_action_hint: Target process startup/config-load overhead rather than lint phases (which are already near-zero on warm cache hits).

description: Switched diagnostic final ordering from stable sort to sort_unstable in linter result paths; parity remains exact (delta 0) and warm runtime improved to 25.78ms on confirmation run.

asi.hypothesis: Stable sorting over thousands of diagnostics adds avoidable overhead; unstable sort should preserve required ordering by key while reducing warm runtime.
asi.change: sorted.sort_by -> sorted.sort_unstable_by in src/linter.rs (2 call sites)

description: Attempted JSON formatter compact-output optimization, but experiment crashed during test build due local disk exhaustion (`No space left on device`).

asi.hypothesis: Switching JSON formatter from pretty to compact serialization should reduce output overhead and improve warm runtime.
asi.rollback_reason: Infrastructure failure: rustc test build failed with ENOSPC before benchmark execution.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun experiment without full test build (or free disk space) and validate parity/runtime directly.
asi.error: rustc-LLVM IO failure on output stream: No space left on device

description: Retried compact JSON serialization (`to_string` instead of pretty) with parity guard; mean warm runtime 26.13ms (delta 0) but gain is marginal/noisy and changes user-visible JSON formatting.

asi.hypothesis: Compact JSON output reduces serialization and write overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Observed improvement is within noise and not worth output-format behavior change risk.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on startup/config/discovery hot paths that can yield larger, stable wins without changing output shape.

description: Made schema initialization lazy (first cache miss only) to avoid warm-hit startup work, but warm runtime regressed to 26.93ms; parity remained exact.

asi.hypothesis: Deferring schema init off warm stat-hit runs would reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed significantly (26.93ms > 26.23ms baseline).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert lazy schema init and focus on other startup costs (discovery/config loading).

description: Measured runtime path difference: forcing `RAYON_NUM_THREADS=8` (global rayon pool) averaged 25.28ms vs default 25.92ms while parity stayed 0; indicates current custom capped pool setup likely adds overhead.

asi.hypothesis: Current custom thread-pool creation path may be slower than using rayon global pool at the same thread count.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change in this measurement-only run.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement code path that configures global rayon pool to capped size once, instead of building a custom local pool.

description: Reworked thread-pool setup to configure rayon global pool (capped to 8) instead of per-run custom pool install, but warm runtime regressed to 26.76ms; parity stayed 0.

asi.hypothesis: Using a global capped rayon pool would reduce custom pool overhead and improve warm runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed materially vs baseline (26.76ms > 26.23ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Abandon pool-initialization refactor and target discovery/config overhead instead.

description: Measured warm runtime sensitivity to thread count: forcing RAYON_NUM_THREADS=1 severely regressed to 38.63ms; default remained ~26.36ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Single-thread execution might reduce scheduler overhead on stat-hit cache runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Measurement disproved hypothesis (thread=1 far slower) and no code change was made.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep parallelism and investigate faster per-file cache-hit path or end-of-run overhead.

description: Skipped cache flush+session eviction on unchanged warm runs by gating them behind `cache.is_dirty()`. Parity stayed exact (delta 0) and warm runtime improved on confirmation to 25.69ms (p50 25.56).

asi.hypothesis: Warm stat-hit runs should avoid filesystem-heavy cache maintenance when no entries changed.
asi.change: add ResultCache::is_dirty(); run flush+evict only when dirty in linter

description: Replaced per-file SHA-256 path key derivation with direct path-string keys to reduce stat-hit lookup cost, but warm runtime regressed to 26.76ms; parity delta stayed 0.

asi.hypothesis: Removing cryptographic path hashing from cache lookups should speed warm stat-hit runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed vs baseline (26.76ms > 26.23ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep hashed path-key approach; investigate other startup costs instead of cache-key derivation.

description: Replaced cache path-key hashing from SHA-256 to DefaultHasher-based 64-bit key (`nitrocop-path-v3`); parity remained exact (delta 0) and warm runtime improved consistently (~25.61-25.62ms).

asi.hypothesis: Per-file SHA-256 path hashing is expensive on warm stat-hit runs; a faster stable non-cryptographic hash should reduce lookup overhead without changing lint results.
asi.change: compute_path_hash uses DefaultHasher instead of Sha256 for path key

description: Changed cache index key type from 16-char hex string to raw u64 hash key to avoid key formatting/allocation overhead, but warm runtime regressed to 26.56ms while parity stayed exact.

asi.hypothesis: Using numeric cache keys (u64) would reduce stat-hit lookup overhead vs String keys.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed (26.56ms > 26.23ms baseline).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert numeric-key experiment and explore other stat-hit materialization costs.

description: Switched cache path hashing input from `path.to_string_lossy()` to `path.hash()` to avoid potential string conversion overhead, but warm runtime was unchanged at 26.23ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Hashing Path directly may be cheaper than hashing lossy path string in per-file cache lookups.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve (26.23ms unchanged vs baseline).
asi.next_action_hint: Try reducing cached diagnostic materialization costs on stat hits instead of hash input micro-optimization.

description: Replaced cache path-key hashing with a lightweight FNV-1a implementation over path bytes (`nitrocop-path-v4`); parity stayed exact (delta 0) and confirmation run improved warm runtime to 25.70ms (p50 25.63).

asi.hypothesis: A custom non-cryptographic path hash can further cut per-file cache lookup overhead beyond DefaultHasher.
asi.change: compute_path_hash now uses FNV-1a over path bytes (unix OsStr bytes, fallback lossy string).

description: Replaced iterator+collect with manual preallocated loops when materializing cached diagnostics on stat/content hits, but warm runtime regressed to 25.81ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Manual Vec::with_capacity + push may reduce iterator overhead in cache-hit diagnostic materialization.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (25.81ms > 25.70ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep iterator approach and target larger startup/discovery overhead instead.

description: Removed skip_summary clone in main lint path by evaluating strict checks from borrowed result state before moving summary into formatter; warm runtime regressed to 26.48ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding SkipSummary clone allocations should shave startup overhead on warm runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current baseline (26.48ms > 26.23ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert clone-elimination attempt and continue targeting cache-hit conversion or config/discovery startup costs.

description: parking_lot RwLock experiment command crashed because rustfmt was invoked on Cargo.toml (`cargo fmt -- src/cache.rs Cargo.toml`), which rustfmt treats as Rust source.

asi.hypothesis: Using parking_lot::RwLock for cache lock hot paths may improve warm stat-hit runtime.
asi.rollback_reason: Experiment command error (tool invocation mistake), not code behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun with `cargo fmt -- src/cache.rs` and then benchmark parity/runtime.

description: Path-caching experiment produced invalid ultra-fast timings because `--cache-clear` removed lockfile and subsequent measured runs exited early (`No lockfile found`). Result is not a real lint runtime.

asi.hypothesis: Caching full file path in cache entries can reduce per-hit path conversion overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark was invalid (measured command failed fast instead of linting).
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun with valid lockfile initialization before timing and reject any run where measured command exits early.
asi.invalid_reason: nitrocop exited with lockfile error after cache-clear

description: Retried cache-entry path caching with valid lockfile setup; storing path in each cache entry and reusing it for hit materialization regressed warm runtime to 26.61ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Persisting entry path string can avoid repeated path conversion on stat/content hits.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed materially (26.61ms > 26.23ms baseline).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert entry-path caching; keep current hit materialization path.

description: Added fast-path `has_lint_failure` helper that avoids severity scan when fail_level is convention, but warm runtime regressed to 25.86ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Default fail-level (`convention`) can short-circuit lint-failure detection with an `is_empty` check instead of iterating all diagnostics.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (25.86ms > 25.70ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert lint-failure fast-path and keep focusing on heavier startup/cache-hit overheads.

description: Swapped cache lock implementation from std::sync::RwLock to parking_lot::RwLock in cache hot path, but warm runtime regressed badly (mean 27.21ms with large spikes) while parity remained exact.

asi.hypothesis: parking_lot::RwLock would reduce lock overhead for cache-hit lookups.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed strongly versus baseline/current best.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert lock implementation change; avoid introducing parking_lot for this path.

description: Switched discovery-cache filename hashing from canonicalized-path SHA-256 to direct-path DefaultHasher (`nitrocop-discovery-v2`) to reduce startup overhead, but warm runtime regressed to 25.84ms; parity remained exact.

asi.hypothesis: Discovery cache key derivation can be cheaper without canonicalization+SHA-256 and improve startup on warm runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (25.84ms > 25.70ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert discovery-cache key change and continue investigating startup costs with profiling-guided larger wins.

description: Switched JSON formatter from `to_string_pretty` allocation to streaming `to_writer_pretty`, but warm runtime regressed dramatically to 34.12ms while parity stayed exact.

asi.hypothesis: Streaming pretty JSON directly to writer would avoid large temporary allocation and improve runtime.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric catastrophically regressed (34.12ms vs ~25.7ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert streaming serializer; keep pre-rendered string path for current performance profile.

description: Tried `serde_json::to_vec_pretty` + write_all for JSON formatter output to avoid intermediate string formatting overhead, but warm runtime regressed to 26.14ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Serializing to bytes then writing directly may be faster than `to_string_pretty` + writeln.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worse than current best (26.14ms > 25.70ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Stop pursuing JSON formatter serialization micro-optimizations; target non-output startup costs.

description: Refactored JSON formatter to serialize borrowed offense/skip data (no per-offense String clones for path/cop/message and no skip vector clones). Parity stayed exact (delta 0) and warm runtime improved to 24.96ms on confirmation.

asi.hypothesis: JSON formatting clones dominate warm runtime; borrowing existing diagnostic/skip strings during serialization should significantly reduce allocation overhead.
asi.change: src/formatter/json.rs uses OffenseRef<'a> and SkippedOutput<'a> with borrowed fields

description: Tried making JSON offense severity a `char` (instead of allocating 1-char String per offense), but warm runtime regressed to 26.33ms while parity remained exact.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding tiny per-offense String allocations for severity should marginally improve JSON formatting cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed significantly versus current best (26.33ms > 24.96ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep severity as String with current borrowed-ref formatter; avoid further JSON field micro-tweaks.

description: Changed JSON offense severity serialization from allocated String to borrowed static string (`&'static str`) via severity mapping helper; parity stayed exact and confirmation run improved warm runtime to 24.70ms (p50 24.66).

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating per-offense severity String allocation in JSON formatter should provide a small but measurable speedup on warm runs.
asi.change: src/formatter/json.rs OffenseRef.severity: String -> &'static str with severity_letter_str()

description: Switched result-cache index persistence from JSON to bincode with JSON fallback for reads. This significantly reduced warm startup/index load overhead; parity stayed exact and confirmation run reached 24.59ms (p50 24.56).

asi.hypothesis: Cache index deserialization is a startup bottleneck; binary serde format should load faster than JSON while retaining compatibility via fallback read.
asi.change: src/cache.rs: flush uses bincode::serialize; load_index tries bincode::deserialize then serde_json fallback
asi.compatibility: existing JSON cache files still readable

description: Switched git discovery cache persistence to bincode with JSON fallback for reads (`src/fs.rs`). This reduced discovery-cache load overhead; parity stayed exact and confirmation run improved warm runtime to 23.87ms (p50 23.86).

asi.hypothesis: Discovery cache JSON parse/write is a startup bottleneck similar to result-cache index; binary serialization should lower overhead while keeping backward compatibility.
asi.change: src/fs.rs load_git_discovery_cache uses bincode deserialize with JSON fallback; write_git_discovery_cache writes bincode
asi.compatibility: existing JSON discovery cache files still readable

description: Extended binary-cache migration to config caches in `src/config/mod.rs`: RuboCop defaults and gem config layer caches now use bincode persistence with JSON fallback reads. Parity stayed exact; confirmation warm runtime improved to 23.45ms (p50 23.49).

asi.hypothesis: Config cache JSON parsing contributes to startup cost; bincode persistence with backward-compatible JSON fallback should reduce warm runtime.
asi.change: load/write cached_rubocop_defaults + cached_gem_config_layer switched to bincode serialize/deserialize with serde_json fallback
asi.compatibility: existing JSON config cache entries remain readable

description: Config-cache key hashing experiment failed to compile due missing `std::hash::{Hash, Hasher}` imports after replacing SHA-256 path hashing code.

asi.hypothesis: Replacing config cache-path SHA-256+canonicalize with lightweight hasher may reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Compilation failure (E0599) from missing trait imports; experiment did not run.
asi.next_action_hint: Add required hash trait imports, remove now-unused sha2 imports, and rerun benchmark with parity guard.
asi.error: no method named `hash` / `finish` found (traits not in scope)

description: Migrated project lockfile persistence to bincode with JSON fallback read in `src/config/lockfile.rs`, but warm runtime measured 24.45ms (parity delta 0), worse than current best after other cache optimizations.

asi.hypothesis: Reading lockfile as binary should reduce startup parse overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert lockfile format change; lockfile parsing is not a dominant warm-path cost now.

description: Profiling-only rerun after recent cache migrations: warm mean measured 24.12ms with parity delta 0 and debug wall clock 21ms; no code change, used to refresh noise floor.

asi.hypothesis: Re-measuring current baseline helps separate real improvements from runtime drift/noise.
asi.rollback_reason: No implementation change in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Target explicit non-cache startup hotspots; collect config-load timings with the profiling env enabled if needed.

description: Tried custom streaming serialization for JSON offenses (serialize_seq over diagnostics) to remove temporary `Vec<OffenseRef>` allocation, but warm runtime regressed to 23.82ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating offenses Vec allocation in formatter will reduce JSON formatting overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed vs current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep simpler borrowed-Vec formatter; sequence serializer added overhead.

description: Replaced lockfile-path cache key SHA-256 with DefaultHasher (`nitrocop-lockfile-v2`) while keeping canonicalization, but warm runtime regressed to 24.16ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Lockfile path hashing with a lightweight hasher would reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened vs current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep existing lockfile path hashing; this path is not a high-impact hotspot.

description: Made autocorrect allowlist loading lazy for non-autocorrect runs (use empty allowlist when autocorrect mode is off), but warm runtime regressed to 23.65ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding JSON parse+HashSet build of autocorrect allowlist on normal lint runs should reduce startup cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert lazy allowlist loading; this setup cost is not a dominant warm-path hotspot.

description: Tried replacing config-cache key SHA-256 with DefaultHasher for cache-path naming. Warm runtime catastrophically regressed to 27.91ms (parity delta 0) because DefaultHasher is process-randomized, causing cache path churn/misses across runs.

asi.hypothesis: Cheaper hash for config cache filenames might reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed heavily and design is invalid for persistent cache keys due randomized hasher seed.
asi.next_action_hint: Never use DefaultHasher for on-disk cache keys; keep stable hash functions for persistent cache paths.
asi.root_cause: DefaultHasher random seed breaks cross-process cache key stability

description: Replaced config-cache key SHA-256 with stable FNV-1a path hash (`*-v4`), but warm runtime regressed severely to 28.16ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Stable non-cryptographic path hashing could lower config-cache key derivation cost while keeping cross-process stability.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed catastrophically; cache-key derivation is not the bottleneck and key migration likely hurt cache reuse.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep existing SHA-256-based config cache keying and avoid further cache-key format churn.

description: Gated noisy `bundle info --path` timing eprintln behind `NITROCOP_DEBUG_BUNDLE_INFO` env var in gem path resolver. Parity stayed exact but warm runtime was 24.44ms, worse than current best.

asi.hypothesis: Unconditional debug stderr writes in gem path resolution add avoidable overhead in normal runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve against current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert this cleanup from the optimization branch and keep searching higher-impact startup costs.

description: Measurement-only comparison of output formats on current code: JSON mean 23.02ms vs quiet mean 23.45ms (delta 0). No implementation change; used to reassess bottlenecks.

asi.hypothesis: If formatter cost still dominates, quiet format should be materially faster than JSON.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Given minimal/no JSON penalty, prioritize non-formatter startup work rather than further JSON micro-optimizations.

description: Removed unnecessary String allocation in TierMap::load for embedded tiers.json path (parse directly from include_str), but warm runtime regressed to 24.22ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding one startup allocation/clone while loading tier metadata can reduce warm runtime.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened vs current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Skip tiny startup allocation tweaks; focus on larger deterministic startup costs.

description: Removed canonicalize() from config cache-path key generation (still SHA-256 based) to avoid filesystem resolution overhead, but warm runtime 23.59ms remained worse than current best.

asi.hypothesis: Canonicalization calls in config cache keying add avoidable startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not beat best (23.59ms > 23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep canonicalization for stable keying; optimize elsewhere.

description: Added formatter hook to pass precomputed corrected_count from linter result (avoiding diagnostics scan in JSON formatter), but warm runtime regressed to 24.17ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Skipping per-run corrected_count scan in formatter will shave JSON output overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert corrected_count plumbing; this scan is not a meaningful hotspot.

description: Added conditional diagnostic sorting (`maybe_sort_diagnostics`) to skip sort when output is already ordered, but warm runtime regressed to 24.13ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Most warm-run diagnostics may already be ordered, so skipping sort could reduce end-of-run cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert conditional sort check; linear pre-check overhead outweighed any sorting savings.

description: Disabled discovery cache usage (always run `git ls-files`) to test whether cache validation overhead dominates, but warm runtime regressed dramatically to 78.27ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Discovery-cache stamp validation might cost more than rerunning git ls-files each invocation.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric catastrophically regressed; discovery cache is essential for warm performance.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep discovery cache and optimize its validation path instead of bypassing it.

description: Gated cache hit/miss atomic counters behind `args.debug` in linter hot path to avoid per-file atomic ops on normal runs, but warm runtime 23.68ms still regressed versus best.

asi.hypothesis: Atomic counter increments for cache stats add measurable overhead on warm stat-hit runs when debug is off.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not beat current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert debug-counter gating optimization; atomic stats not a dominant cost after recent cache/formatter improvements.

description: Changed config cache path hashing to use raw OS bytes on unix (avoiding lossy string conversion) while keeping SHA-256, but measured warm runtime regressed heavily to 44.56ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Using OsStr bytes for SHA-256 path hashing in config cache keying may reduce allocation overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric catastrophically worsened in this run; not safe to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert OS-bytes hash input tweak and avoid further config cache keying changes.

description: Fail-fast branch split experiment crashed during release build due disk exhaustion (`No space left on device`) before benchmark execution.

asi.hypothesis: Separate fail_fast and non-fail_fast parallel loops to remove per-file branch/atomic checks on normal runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Infrastructure failure (ENOSPC) during compile prevented performance measurement.
asi.next_action_hint: Free disk space and rerun build+benchmark for this change, or use existing release binary with lighter rebuild path.
asi.error: rustc-LLVM IO failure: No space left on device

description: Baseline remeasurement after ENOSPC event showed a heavy one-off outlier (546.9ms) with normal samples ~23.3–24.5ms; mean degraded to 45.66ms while parity remained 0.

asi.hypothesis: Re-establish runtime baseline before next optimization after build crash.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened due severe outlier; no code change.
asi.next_action_hint: Use robust statistic (p50/trimmed mean) for diagnosis and discard outlier-affected no-change runs.

description: Re-ran fail-fast branch split optimization after freeing disk space; separating fail_fast and non-fail_fast rayon loops kept parity but warm runtime was 24.03ms (worse than current best).

asi.hypothesis: Removing per-file `args.fail_fast` branch/atomic checks in the non-fail-fast path improves hot-loop throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not beat current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert branch split and avoid further micro-branch refactors in linter loop.

description: Reduced discovery cache watched-dir set to immediate parents only (instead of all ancestors) to cut stamp validation stats, but warm runtime regressed to 24.55ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Fewer watched directory mtimes should reduce discovery cache validation overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert watched-dir reduction; broad parent tracking may be helping cache stability and avoiding costly rediscovery.

description: Replaced `writeln!("{}", json)` with direct `write_all` calls in JSON formatter to avoid formatting machinery overhead, but warm runtime regressed to 23.97ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Direct byte writes after to_string_pretty should be faster than writeln formatting.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep existing writeln path; formatter write-call micro-optimizations are not paying off.

description: Removed SkipSummary clone in both stdin and directory paths by computing strict checks before moving summary into formatter, but warm runtime regressed to 24.55ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding SkipSummary cloning should reduce small startup allocations on warm runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert clone-elimination refactor; clone cost is not a dominant hotspot.

description: Removed unconditional `bundle info --path` timing eprintln in gem path resolver, but warm runtime regressed to 23.67ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating always-on debug stderr formatting in gem resolution should reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not beat current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep existing gem-path logging behavior; this path is not a dominant bottleneck.

description: Removed canonicalize() from discovery cache path key derivation (kept SHA-256), but warm runtime regressed to 24.07ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Skipping directory canonicalization in discovery cache key generation may reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep canonicalization for discovery cache key stability; avoid further cache-key derivation tweaks.

description: Measurement-only rerun on current baseline comparing default JSON vs `--quiet-skips`: means were effectively identical (22.98ms vs 23.00ms), parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Skip-summary stderr output may still be a meaningful warm-path cost after recent optimizations.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; observed difference (~0.03ms) is noise-level and not actionable.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue quiet-skips behavior changes for perf; focus on non-I/O startup hotspots.

description: Made SkipSummary cloning conditional on strict-mode usage in stdin and directory paths to avoid default-run clone allocations, but warm runtime regressed to 24.20ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Most runs do not use --strict, so avoiding unconditional skip-summary clone should reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert conditional clone logic; this allocation is not a significant hotspot.

description: Merged corrected_count calculation into the offense-construction loop in JSON formatter (single pass over diagnostics), but warm runtime regressed to 23.72ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Combining corrected_count and offense mapping into one loop should reduce formatter overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert one-pass JSON loop; existing iterator-based approach performs better.

description: Added best-effort JSON→bincode migration-on-read for result-cache index to avoid repeated fallback parse on legacy files, but warm runtime remained worse than current best (23.67ms, parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Auto-migrating legacy JSON cache index files to bincode on read should reduce recurring parse overhead on warm runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve vs current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Do not add migration-on-read complexity for this path; gains are negligible on current workloads.

description: Added unix MetadataExt fast-path for discovery mtime extraction to bypass SystemTime conversion, but warm runtime regressed to 23.83ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Using raw unix mtime/mtime_nsec avoids conversion overhead during discovery cache stamp validation.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert unix mtime fast-path; conversion overhead is not the dominant factor.

description: Added fast lockfile freshness stamp check (mtime+nanos+size) to skip Gemfile.lock SHA-256 hashing when unchanged, but warm runtime regressed to 24.26ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding per-run Gemfile.lock content hashing in lockfile freshness check should reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened vs current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert lockfile stamp fast-path; lockfile hashing is not a dominant warm-path cost in this workload.

description: Changed non-fail-fast linter path to collect per-file diagnostics in file order and skip global sort (kept sort for fail-fast). Output determinism check passed, parity stayed exact, but warm runtime regressed to 24.15ms.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding full diagnostic sort in normal runs by preserving file-order collection should reduce end-of-run overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert sort-elision approach; current flat_map+sort pipeline is faster in practice.

description: Switched result-cache path hashing from FNV-1a to xxHash64 (stable seeded) for faster keying, but warm runtime regressed to 23.95ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: xxHash64 may outperform FNV-1a for per-file cache key derivation in warm stat-hit runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert xxHash attempt and keep FNV-1a path hashing.

description: Gated bundle-path debug logging behind env flag with OnceLock-cached check (to avoid unconditional eprintln), but warm runtime regressed to 24.17ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Suppressing always-on bundle info timing logs with a cheap one-time env check will reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert logging gate; bundle-path logging is not the limiting warm-path cost.

description: Replaced `format!("{hash:016x}")` with manual fixed-width hex encoding for cache path hash strings to reduce formatting overhead, but warm runtime regressed to 24.13ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Manual hex encoding should be cheaper than format! for per-file cache key generation.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep simple format! path; hash-string formatting is not a dominant cost.

description: No-change baseline characterization over 48 warm samples: mean 25.53ms, p50 24.49ms, trimmed mean 25.05ms, with heavy tail outliers up to 34.60ms. Parity remained exact.

asi.hypothesis: Longer sampling will better characterize current noise/outlier profile before further micro-optimizations.
asi.rollback_reason: No implementation change; run used for measurement only and showed significant tail noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Use robust statistics (p50/trimmed mean) to judge micro-optimizations and avoid reacting to outlier-driven mean shifts.

description: Tried parallel sort for large diagnostic vectors (`par_sort_unstable_by` when len>1024) and confirmed parity stayed exact, but follow-up run measured 23.65ms so no reliable gain over current best.

asi.hypothesis: Parallel sorting may reduce end-of-run sorting cost when offense count is large.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation run did not improve primary metric versus existing best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert parallel sort threshold tweak; sort phase is not dominant enough here.

description: No-change long-run baseline characterization (60 warm samples) after recent keeps: mean 23.42ms, p50 23.14ms, trimmed mean 23.28ms, parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: A longer sample window can establish a robust post-optimization baseline and noise envelope.
asi.rollback_reason: No implementation change in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Evaluate future changes against p50/trimmed-mean deltas >=0.3ms to avoid chasing noise.

description: Gated gem-path timing log behind `NITROCOP_DEBUG_BUNDLE_INFO` env var (no default stderr spam), but 48-sample warm run still measured 23.51ms mean with parity delta 0, not better than current best.

asi.hypothesis: Suppressing unconditional bundle info timing eprintln reduces startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not beat current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert logging gate optimization and avoid further low-impact stderr tweaks.

description: Added lockfile freshness fast-path using stored Gemfile.lock (mtime_secs,size) to skip SHA-256 when unchanged, but warm runtime regressed to 23.83ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Coarse lockfile stamp check can avoid expensive hash_file on hot runs and reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert lockfile stamp fast-path; lockfile SHA-256 check is not dominating warm runtime.

description: Avoided canonicalize() for already-absolute config cache key paths via `stable_cache_key_path()`, but warm runtime regressed to 23.75ms (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Skipping unnecessary canonicalization for absolute paths should reduce startup overhead while preserving cache key stability.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert absolute-path canonicalization shortcut; path canonicalization is not a dominant bottleneck here.

description: Removed unconditional bundle timing instrumentation/eprintln from gem path resolution hot path. Parity stayed exact, but 48-sample warm mean was 23.58ms (p50 23.50), not better than current best.

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating per-gem Instant+eprintln overhead in gem path resolution should reduce warm startup time.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not beat current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Do not spend more cycles on gem-path debug logging micro-optimizations; move to higher-level startup hotspots.

description: Refactored gem-path cache keying from `(working_dir, gem_name)` tuple keys to per-working-dir `HashMap<String, PathBuf>` to avoid per-lookup key allocations on warm hits. Parity stayed exact, but warm mean was 23.62ms (p50 23.51), not better than best.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding tuple key allocation in resolve_gem_path cache hits should reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not beat current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid further gem-path keying micro-optimizations; prioritize larger startup phases.

description: No-change profiling pass with one `--debug` run showed warm cache behavior (6101 stat hits, 0 misses, linter wall clock 13ms, config load 3ms). Timing-only 24-sample follow-up measured 22.93ms mean (parity delta 0).

asi.hypothesis: Capture current hot-path breakdown to target non-cache startup overhead after recent cache wins.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; measurement-only run.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on process/startup costs outside linter core, which is already ~13ms wall on warm hits.

description: Switched embedded JSON loads to byte-based deserialization (`serde_json::from_slice(include_bytes!(...))`) for tiers and autocorrect allowlist to reduce startup allocations, but warm runtime was 23.51ms with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Parsing embedded resources from static bytes instead of allocating intermediate Strings should reduce process startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not beat current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Treat embedded JSON parse/allocation as low impact; focus on larger startup components.

description: Attempted compact JSON output via `serde_json::to_writer` + newline in formatter, but build failed with borrow checker error (moved `out` then reused for newline).

asi.hypothesis: Compact JSON serialization could reduce output generation overhead versus pretty JSON.
asi.rollback_reason: Compilation failed (E0382: value borrowed after move in formatter write path).
asi.next_action_hint: Fix by reborrowing writer (`&mut *out`) for `to_writer`, then rerun benchmark before deciding.

description: Retried JSON formatter switch to `serde_json::to_writer` (compact output + newline) to avoid building pretty string, but warm runtime regressed heavily to 25.29ms despite parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Streaming compact JSON directly to writer would reduce allocation/copy cost in JSON formatting.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed significantly versus current best (~23.45ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep existing `to_string_pretty` formatter path; streaming writer path introduces costly write-call overhead.

description: Tried compact JSON string serialization (`serde_json::to_string`) instead of pretty JSON in formatter. Parity stayed exact, but 48-sample warm runtime regressed to 24.12ms (p50 24.14).

asi.hypothesis: Compact JSON should reduce formatter CPU by emitting fewer bytes than pretty JSON.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms class / recent 22.93 baseline sample).
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit compact JSON output paths; keep pretty-string formatter implementation.

description: Lazily skipped autocorrect allowlist JSON parsing when autocorrect mode is Off (new empty allowlist path), but warm runtime regressed to 24.70ms (p50 24.59) with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding allowlist parse+HashSet build on non-autocorrect runs should reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened significantly versus current best (~23.45ms class).
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue allowlist lazy-load micro-optimizations further; impact is dominated by other startup costs.

description: Bypassed full tier map JSON load for `--preview` runs via new `TierMap::preview_default()` (empty overrides, default preview tier). Parity stayed exact, but warm runtime regressed to 23.80ms (p50 23.76).

asi.hypothesis: Skipping tiers.json parsing/allocation in preview mode should reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best (~23.45ms class).
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue tier-map lazy/default shortcuts; tiers load cost is not the dominant bottleneck.

description: No-change profiling run with `NITROCOP_PROFILE_CONFIG=1` plus debug to inspect startup phases; warm follow-up measured 23.07ms (p50 23.05) with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Extra config profiling output may reveal actionable non-linter startup hotspots after cache optimizations.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; measurement-only run.
asi.next_action_hint: Add finer-grained instrumentation around discovery + formatter/output phases; current debug output still too coarse.

description: No-change format-cost characterization: JSON warm mean 22.91ms vs quiet 23.23ms (format overhead estimate -0.33ms), parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Quantify JSON formatter contribution to warm runtime before further formatter work.
asi.rollback_reason: Measurement-only run; no implementation change.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not assume formatter dominates warm cost; focus on discovery/config/cache conversion hotspots.

description: Switched discovery cache filename hashing from SHA-256 to stable FNV-1a (`nitrocop-discovery-v2`) to cut startup hash work; parity stayed exact. Confirmation run was 22.92ms (p50 22.88), essentially noise-level versus recent no-change baseline (~22.91ms).

asi.hypothesis: Cheaper stable hash for discovery cache path keying should reduce warm startup overhead without changing behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: No clear primary-metric gain over recent no-change baseline; result is indistinguishable from noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid further cache-key-format churn unless paired with a larger structural startup reduction.

description: Tried skipping global `files/sub_config_dirs` sort+dedup in discovery for single-directory targets (`nitrocop .`) to trim startup work. Parity stayed exact and output determinism check passed, but confirmation run measured 23.54ms (p50 23.55), no reliable gain.

asi.hypothesis: Single-directory discovery already yields stable unique vectors, so bypassing final sort/dedup may reduce warm startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation run did not beat current performance envelope; result appears noise-level/regressive.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid further single-dir sort/dedup micro-tuning in discovery and target larger startup phases.

description: Optimized result-cache session hash computation (`nitrocop-session-v5`) with default-config fast path and reduced formatting/cloning for empty include/exclude/options, but warm runtime regressed to 24.24ms (p50 24.20) with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Session-hash generation over ~900 cop configs is startup-heavy; fast-pathing default configs should reduce cache initialization cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened significantly versus current best envelope.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid further session-hash micro-tuning unless accompanied by direct profiling proof; likely overshadowed by other startup costs.

description: Used unix `MetadataExt` mtime extraction in result-cache stat/content/put paths to avoid `SystemTime` conversion on each metadata read. Parity stayed exact, but warm mean was 23.68ms (p50 23.45, trim 23.49) with a tail outlier.

asi.hypothesis: Direct unix mtime/mtime_nsec reads in hot cache metadata paths should reduce per-file stat-hit overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not show reliable improvement versus current best envelope; mean regressed and robust stats were neutral.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit mtime conversion micro-optimizations; focus on larger startup phases beyond per-file metadata conversion.

description: Split linter parallel file loop into explicit fail-fast and non-fail-fast branches to remove per-file fail-fast branch checks on normal runs. Parity stayed exact, but warm runtime regressed to 23.76ms (p50 23.76).

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating repeated `args.fail_fast` conditionals in the hot per-file closure should slightly improve warm throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best envelope.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid further fail-fast branch micro-optimizations in the linter loop.

description: Optimized global exclude matching by deduplicating base_dir/config_dir strip-prefix checks when both dirs are identical, but warm runtime regressed to 23.79ms (p50 23.84) with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Many files pay duplicate relative-path/regex checks when base_dir==config_dir; collapsing to one pass should reduce per-file overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best envelope.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid further global-exclude dedup micro-tweaks; prioritize larger startup hotspots.

description: Added early-return guard in `schema::init` to skip repeated `SCHEMA_ROOT.set(...)` work once initialized, but warm runtime regressed to 23.60ms (p50 23.58) with parity delta 0.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding repeat schema-root initialization mapping/allocation in hot command path can reduce startup overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best envelope.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue schema init micro-optimizations; target larger startup phases.

description: No-change long-run characterization (72 warm samples) to re-establish current noise envelope after many reversions; parity remained exact but mean drifted to 24.48ms.

asi.hypothesis: A larger sample run will reveal whether recent apparent wins were stable or noise-driven.
asi.rollback_reason: Measurement-only run with no implementation change; primary metric not an improvement run.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat current environment as shifted/noisy and require repeated confirmations before keeping micro-optimizations.

description: Added `has_migrated_schema_version()` fast guard so migrated-file basename scanning runs only when `AllCops.MigratedSchemaVersion` is configured. Parity stayed exact, but warm runtime regressed to 23.68ms (p50 23.59).

asi.hypothesis: Skipping per-file migration filename scanning when migrated schema version is unset should reduce hot-path overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus current best envelope.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid further migration-skip micro-guards; impact is too small/noisy.
